title: Component Implementations
text: Component Implementations The component signature specifies the implementation a component, which determines the technology used to deploy it, such as Uniface, C, or Java. In theory, a component may have more than one implementation, making it possible for users to choose the implementation they want to use when the application is deployed. In this case, you can define a single signature that lists both implementations. A component interface alone is not sufficient to deliver functionality. The functionality of a component is realized by specifying the implementation of the component. You can specify the component implementation using any of the Uniface component editors, or using another component construction tool. Uniface provides call-in and call-out mechanisms that link the component into an application. The implementations you choose affect the operations that are available. If a component has multiple implementations, you can specify a default implementation. At runtime, the default implementation is executed, unless the [SERVICES_EXEC] section of the usys.asn file specifies that another implementation should be used. Table 1. Component Implementations Implementation type Operations available by default Editable Comments Uniface Component ACCEPT , EXEC , QUIT Y These are built-in Uniface operations. You can define parameters for the EXEC operation only. INIT , CLEANUP These are explicit operations. If you define operations named INIT and CLEANUP they are activated implicitly. For more information on INIT and CLEANUP see operation . You cannot define parameters for INIT and CLEANUP . C None N You can define the default operations ACCEPT , QUIT , and EXEC and the implicit operations INIT and CLEANUP . You cannot define parameters for ACCEPT , QUIT , INIT or CLEANUP . However, if you define both 4GL and C implementations for a component, the ACCEPT , QUIT , and EXEC parameters become available. If you then delete the 4GL implementation, these parameters remain available for the C implementation. COM None N No default operations are available. Java Call-in None N No default operations are available. Stored Procedure None N No default operations are available. Operating System Service COMMAND , COMMANDOUT N Enter operating system commands using ProcScript to be executed via an Operating System Service. You can use your assignment file to redirect the Operating System Service components to a Uniface server. The maximum length of a command is 511 bytes. Related Links Integrating with Other Technologies The Uniface architecture enables you to integrate Uniface and non-Uniface components in a single application, and to deploy the application on a large variety of platforms and technologies. Define a Component Implementation Each component can have one or more implementations. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
